title: It Had To Be You

jokes.text: In that case, I want to marry you and have your child. / I'm down with that, too.
jokes.setup: Hilary trying to scare Jazz off by escalating her rejection
jokes.punchline: Jazz being completely undeterred and agreeing to marriage and kids
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven humor showing Jazz's obliviousness and Hilary's exasperation. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: I got a great view of her running out.
jokes.setup: Jazz apologizing for Hilary running away
jokes.punchline: Jazz finding the silver lining in her rejection
jokes.explanation: Solid character beat showing Jazz's sleazy optimism even in rejection.

jokes.text: How many times you helped me out? / Seven.
jokes.setup: Will's rhetorical question about Jazz helping him out
jokes.punchline: Jazz's unexpectedly specific low number
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure. The specific number 'seven' is unexpectedly precise for what should be a vague grateful response.

jokes.text: Good looks run in my family.
jokes.setup: Will asking if Jazz's sister looks good
jokes.punchline: Jazz claiming good looks run in his family despite his own appearance
jokes.explanation: Standard self-deprecating humor that relies on Jazz's established appearance as the punchline.

jokes.text: your sister should be like a sister to me, not a girlfriend... I'm willing to take that risk.
jokes.setup: Will explaining why he can't date Jazz's sister using friendship logic
jokes.punchline: Jazz completely missing the point and willing to risk their friendship
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Jazz's single-minded focus and complete misunderstanding of Will's careful reasoning.

jokes.text: This budget thing is pretty easy.
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining he spent five times more than he made
jokes.punchline: His conclusion that budgeting is easy despite complete failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his privileged naivety and complete misunderstanding of basic financial concepts.

jokes.text: Much worse.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil hoping it's not the police at the door
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey declaring Jazz worse than police
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry delivery and the implication that Jazz is worse than police involvement is a strong comedic beat.

jokes.text: $10 says I ain't home. / It doesn't quite say it loudly enough. / Do I hear $15?
jokes.setup: Will trying to pay Geoffrey to say he's not home
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey negotiating the bribe price up like an auctioneer
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with Geoffrey's auction-style negotiation. Perfect character work showing his dry wit and opportunism.

jokes.text: Nice to meet you, Dad.
jokes.setup: Janet meeting Uncle Phil for the first time
jokes.punchline: Immediately calling him 'Dad' as if they're already family
jokes.explanation: Strong setup for Janet's overly presumptuous character - immediately assuming family status.

jokes.text: You don't have a goatee.
jokes.setup: Janet meeting Will for the first time
jokes.punchline: Her first comment being about his lack of facial hair
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd and specific first comment that establishes Janet's odd priorities and expectations.

jokes.text: Hey, meet me again.
jokes.setup: Standard introductions being made
jokes.punchline: Hilary's self-absorbed request for a second introduction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment showing her vanity and need for attention even in basic social interactions.

jokes.text: May I get you anything, Master William? A dribble bib?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey offering refreshments to guests
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Will needs a bib like a baby
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's subtle dig at Will delivered with perfect butler formality. The specific suggestion of a 'dribble bib' is wonderfully condescending.

jokes.text: We certainly know who got the brains in your family. / Yeah, but Janet ain't stupid.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil complimenting Janet's intelligence after learning about her scholarship
jokes.punchline: Jazz completely missing that this was an insult to him
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline with Jazz's obliviousness creating the humor. Perfect character integration showing Jazz completely missing social subtext.

jokes.text: Jazz tells me you're a professional wrestler. / I'm afraid he was mistaken. I'm a lawyer. / My apologies. I was misquoted.
jokes.setup: Janet relaying Jazz's description of Uncle Phil as a wrestler
jokes.punchline: Jazz claiming he was 'misquoted' rather than just wrong
jokes.explanation: Good escalating sequence with Jazz's refusal to admit error, using journalism terminology inappropriately.

jokes.text: Well, these aren't used.
jokes.setup: Janet mentioning she was thinking of buying used books
jokes.punchline: Hilary implying her books aren't used because she never opened them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - accidentally revealing her academic laziness while trying to be helpful. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: When I come back, I want you gone. / Guess I got plenty of time then.
jokes.setup: Will telling Jazz to leave while he gets a snack
jokes.punchline: Jazz implying Will eats so much it'll take forever
jokes.explanation: Solid comeback from Jazz, using Will's eating habits as ammunition. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: You know, it's probably not too late to get that spinach out of your teeth.
jokes.setup: Will being confident about Janet liking him
jokes.punchline: Jazz pointing out Will has spinach in his teeth
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag undermining Will's confidence. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Brother, do the words 'boing-yoing-yoing' mean anything to you?
jokes.setup: Jazz asking what Will thinks of Janet
jokes.punchline: Will's cartoonish sound effects to describe his attraction
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd and memorable way to express attraction. The sound effects are creative and quotable.

jokes.text: I'm willing to take that risk. / In that case, how much are you willing to give?
jokes.setup: Will using Jazz's earlier line about taking risks
jokes.punchline: Jazz immediately asking for payment to arrange the date
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure with Jazz's mercenary nature revealed. Shows his opportunism perfectly.

jokes.text: Makes me question the whole theory of genetics.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey commenting that Janet is charming
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil wondering how she and Jazz are related
jokes.explanation: Smart wordplay with scientific terminology to express disbelief about Jazz and Janet being siblings.

jokes.text: You'd be a great dad. / You, too, baby.
jokes.setup: Janet saying Will would be a great father
jokes.punchline: Will accidentally agreeing she'd also be a great dad
jokes.explanation: Simple mistake humor with Will not paying attention to the conversation and giving an inappropriate automatic response.

jokes.text: And I never will be again.
jokes.setup: Janet saying she hopes it wasn't the leather jacket that attracted her
jokes.punchline: Will immediately swearing off the jacket forever
jokes.explanation: Shows Will being overly accommodating and eager to please, but fairly standard relationship humor.

jokes.text: Oh, man.
jokes.setup: Janet declaring Will is 'her man' on their first date
jokes.punchline: Will's quiet realization of what he's gotten into
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Will's dawning realization that Janet is moving too fast. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: You're right. We are going to be great together.
jokes.setup: Janet declining expensive food because they're too expensive
jokes.punchline: Will thinking this means they're compatible financially
jokes.explanation: Will misreading Janet's frugality as compatibility when she's actually planning their entire future together.

jokes.text: We certainly are. / Not. / Then I'll come back.
jokes.setup: Waitress asking if they're ready to order, Will saying yes
jokes.punchline: Will immediately contradicting himself with 'Not' and waitress's matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with the waitress's professional patience in dealing with indecisive customers.

jokes.text: put those googly eyes back in your head
jokes.setup: Janet catching Will looking at the waitress
jokes.punchline: Her colorful description of his wandering eyes
jokes.explanation: Vivid and memorable phrase showing Janet's possessiveness. 'Googly eyes' is a colorful, quotable expression.

jokes.text: No. He'll have the same thing I'm having.
jokes.setup: Will trying to order his own food
jokes.punchline: Janet ordering for him without his consent
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation of Janet's controlling behavior. Good cringe comedy as she takes over basic decisions.

jokes.text: Six kids? / That's right, baby. Three girls, three boys. And don't even think about naming one of them William.
jokes.setup: Janet mentioning six kids casually
jokes.punchline: Her specific family planning including rejecting Will's name for their hypothetical children
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Janet's complete presumption about their future, including rejecting his own name. Perfect character work.

jokes.text: Did you think you could sample the milk without putting a down payment on the cow?
jokes.setup: Janet confronting Will about his intentions
jokes.punchline: Her creative twist on the 'buy the cow' saying with financial terminology
jokes.explanation: Creative variation on a classic saying with business terminology. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: I ordered lemonade.
jokes.setup: Janet's elaborate milk/cow metaphor
jokes.punchline: Will's literal response about his actual beverage order
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection and deadpan delivery. Will's literal interpretation of the metaphor is perfectly timed and shows his comedic instincts. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: And look at me when I'm talking to you!
jokes.setup: Janet's continued controlling behavior
jokes.punchline: Her demanding Will's visual attention like a parent to a child
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Janet's controlling behavior, treating Will like a child. Effective cringe comedy.

jokes.text: I'm glad you found a time that was convenient for you to call.
jokes.setup: Michael calling Ashley as promised
jokes.punchline: Hilary's passive-aggressive greeting
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hilary's dramatic and passive-aggressive nature when dealing with perceived slights.

jokes.text: Yeah, that'll be happening, Mr. God's Gift to the Fifth Grade.
jokes.setup: Michael asking for Ashley to call back
jokes.punchline: Hilary's sarcastic dismissal with age-appropriate insult
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that's age-appropriate for the target but shows Hilary's disdain. Good character integration and quotable phrase.

jokes.text: Yeah. We got any more of those pecan rolls?
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv praising their healthy morning walk
jokes.punchline: Will immediately asking for unhealthy pastries
jokes.explanation: Standard irony showing Will undermining the healthy activity with unhealthy food choices. Solid but predictable.

jokes.text: I'm telling you, if we had a bunny, dude would be on the stove right now.
jokes.setup: Will comparing his situation to Fatal Attraction
jokes.punchline: Referencing the movie's famous bunny-boiling scene
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that's specific and dark. Effectively communicates Will's fear of Janet's intensity.

jokes.text: Like that.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil trying to explain women getting bossy
jokes.punchline: Presumably Aunt Viv giving him a threatening look
jokes.explanation: Good visual beat with Uncle Phil immediately experiencing what he was trying to describe. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: I had to pawn her off on some sucker.
jokes.setup: Jazz revealing he knows his sister is bossy
jokes.punchline: Admitting he deliberately set Will up to get rid of her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz moment showing his selfishness and willingness to sacrifice friends for his own comfort. Great character revelation.

jokes.text: Between you and the Humpty Dance, I'll get a metal plate put in my butt.
jokes.setup: Jazz complaining about his hip injury from dancing
jokes.punchline: His specific and absurd description of the potential medical consequence
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd imagery. The combination of dance reference and medical consequence is memorably ridiculous.

jokes.text: it wasn't pretty. / Dude, he's walking and all, but still.
jokes.setup: Jazz warning about what happens when someone breaks up with Janet
jokes.punchline: Implying serious but unspecified physical harm
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy with vague but ominous implications. The 'walking and all, but still' suggests lasting damage.

jokes.text: I say, marry her. - You can stop that.
jokes.setup: Jazz suggests Will should marry Janet
jokes.punchline: Will immediately shuts down the suggestion
jokes.explanation: Standard setup and shutdown, functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: I ain't living like that. What guy deserves a woman like your sister?
jokes.setup: Will refuses to pass Janet off to another guy
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks what guy deserves such treatment
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Carlton's entrance, shows Will's moral struggle

jokes.text: They're not half bad.
jokes.setup: Carlton enters having borrowed Public Enemy tape
jokes.punchline: Gives lukewarm review of hardcore rap group
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton understatement about revolutionary rap music

jokes.text: That used to be my favorite song.
jokes.setup: Carlton borrowing Will's Public Enemy tape and saying they're 'not half bad'
jokes.punchline: The implication that Carlton has somehow ruined the song for Will
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton/Will dynamic. Carlton's enthusiasm for something immediately makes it uncool. Great economy and character work.

jokes.text: Carlton, never bring a sandwich to a buffet.
jokes.setup: Carlton worrying about not having a date
jokes.punchline: Jazz's crude but memorable dating advice
jokes.explanation: Highly original and memorable metaphor. Crude but cleverly constructed advice that became a quotable phrase.

jokes.text: I have a sister named Jane, but we call her Amy.
jokes.setup: Being introduced to Janet
jokes.punchline: Toni's completely illogical family naming story
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd non-sequitur that establishes Toni as scatterbrained. Random but memorable detail.

jokes.text: And a little further down on the evolutionary chart, Jazz.
jokes.setup: Carlton introducing everyone at the table
jokes.punchline: His scientific put-down of Jazz
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton insult using scientific terminology. Shows his intellectual superiority complex and disdain for Jazz.

jokes.text: Do I look like I care?
jokes.setup: Carlton mentioning Toni is his cousin's best friend
jokes.punchline: Toni's blunt dismissal of social connections
jokes.explanation: Sharp, direct response that establishes Toni's no-nonsense attitude. Good economy and delivery.

jokes.text: Break out the Midol.
jokes.setup: Janet's increasingly cranky behavior
jokes.punchline: Toni's crude suggestion about Janet's mood
jokes.explanation: Crude and somewhat dated humor relying on gender stereotypes. Direct but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Maybe later. We have to see if anyone better is here first.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking if the ladies want to join them
jokes.punchline: Toni's brutally honest reason for declining
jokes.explanation: Brutal honesty delivered casually. Establishes Toni as someone who says exactly what she thinks.

jokes.text: I don't know. You tell us, Carlton.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if that's Patrick Swayze
jokes.punchline: Toni implying Carlton looks like Patrick Swayze
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about Carlton's appearance or mannerisms. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Does he know Janet Jackson?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Carlton knowing celebrities
jokes.punchline: Jazz asks about Janet Jackson while sitting with Janet
jokes.explanation: Name connection joke that's mildly amusing but predictable

jokes.text: Someone has her rude hat on tonight.
jokes.setup: Janet's increasingly rude behavior
jokes.punchline: Carlton's polite but pointed observation
jokes.explanation: Carlton's polite way of calling out bad behavior. Creative metaphor that fits his proper speaking style.

jokes.text: You need more ice, please.
jokes.setup: Janet rudely demanding more ice
jokes.punchline: Carlton correcting her manners by adding 'please'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment showing his commitment to proper manners even in tense situations. Good setup for his big speech.

jokes.text: Janet, your behavior this evening has been completely unacceptable... Do I make myself clear?
jokes.setup: Janet's continued rude behavior throughout the evening
jokes.punchline: Carlton's formal, parental lecture to someone he just met
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment where Carlton's proper upbringing and moral code override social awkwardness. Perfect escalation and delivery.

jokes.text: You know, I'm going to miss him.
jokes.setup: Carlton standing up to Janet so firmly
jokes.punchline: Will assuming Carlton won't survive Janet's wrath
jokes.explanation: Great reaction beat showing Will's shock at Carlton's boldness and fear of the consequences. Perfect timing and character work.

jokes.text: I just say no in a strong, masculine voice. And when I say no, she jumps.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil explaining how he handles Aunt Viv's bossiness
jokes.punchline: His confident claim about his authority
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Uncle Phil's comeuppance. His overconfidence about marital dynamics creates dramatic irony.

jokes.text: What did you say? / I don't know. How long have you been there?
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv catching Uncle Phil's boastful comment
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's immediate panic and attempt to gauge how much she heard
jokes.explanation: Perfect marital comedy showing Uncle Phil's immediate deflation. Great structure and timing.

jokes.text: Philip, you better sleep with one eye open.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil's failed attempt to downplay his comments
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv's ominous threat
jokes.explanation: Classic marital threat delivered with mock menace. Completes the reversal of Uncle Phil's earlier confidence.

jokes.text: I got her just where I want her.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv's threatening departure
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's desperate attempt to maintain face
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Uncle Phil's inability to admit defeat. Classic male pride comedy.

jokes.text: Every woman wants a man to take control... Rib tips?
jokes.setup: Will asking what every woman wants
jokes.punchline: Jazz's food-focused response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz response showing his simple worldview and food obsession. Good misdirection from Will's serious question.

jokes.text: I'll hand-deliver them.
jokes.setup: Carlton correcting Janet about mailing apology notes
jokes.punchline: Janet's immediate, submissive compliance
jokes.explanation: Shows the complete transformation in Janet's behavior after Carlton's lecture. Good character development beat.

jokes.text: I really should write a book.
jokes.setup: Carlton successfully taming Janet
jokes.punchline: His pompous conclusion about his expertise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment of self-satisfaction and intellectual superiority. His pompousness is endearing in context.

jokes.text: The only thing clear about you is the space between your ears.
jokes.setup: Will trying to assert dominance by asking 'Do I make myself clear?'
jokes.punchline: Janet's creative insult about Will's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay using Will's 'clear' question against him. Creative insult that's memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: You got the theory right. You just ain't got the technique.
jokes.setup: Will failing to control Janet the way Carlton did
jokes.punchline: Jazz's analysis of Will's failed attempt
jokes.explanation: Good analysis humor with Jazz breaking down Will's failure. Shows Jazz has observed the pattern but can't execute it himself.

jokes.text: Come here.
jokes.setup: Jazz perfectly recreating Carlton's lecture to Hilary
jokes.punchline: Hilary's immediate positive response
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing the technique works on Hilary too. Excellent economy with just two words conveying the complete reversal.

tmdb_overview: Will has girl problems after a date with Jazz's domineering sister, who's ready to pick out a china pattern before the night is over.
